summary: Iteration 6 frontend-only UI verification of Admin login + Admin console + force-delete visibility for kevzvilbar@gmail.com (Admin). Login at /auth SUCCEEDS (Supabase /auth/v1/token returns 200, no error toast, navigates away from /auth) but is then REDIRECTED to /onboarding 'Complete your profile' — exactly the regression flagged in the review request. Backend Supabase data is correct (user_profiles row: username='Kevz', first_name='Kevin', last_name='Vilbar', designation='Admin', status='Active', plant_assignments=4 plants; user_roles: [{role:'Admin'}]). Direct navigation to /admin BYPASSES the onboarding redirect and renders the console fully (admin-page testid present, no admin-access-denied, 'Admin console' heading visible, Users tab enabled, 2 user rows with delete-menu-trigger-user-* visible). However, after opening the row menu and clicking hard-delete-user-{id} (the 'Permanently delete…' item), the force-hard-delete button is NOT present in the DOM (count=0) — so the visibility-only check for data-testid='force-hard-delete' FAILS. Cancel/Escape was used; no actual delete attempted.

minor.issue: Frontend hits this table on login but it does not exist (PGRST205 'Could not find the table public.login_attempts'). 404 noise in console — table missing from schema.
400 '42703 column power_readings.daily_solar_kwh does not exist'. Dashboard query references columns that aren't in the schema.

ui_bugs.component: Auth.tsx post-login redirect / ProtectedRoute profile-completeness check
ui_bugs.issue: Admin kevzvilbar@gmail.com is forced to /onboarding immediately after successful sign-in even though user_profiles.status='Active', designation='Admin', username/first/last name all populated, and plant_assignments has 4 plants. The 'profile_complete' gate in the post-login routing is mis-evaluating an already-complete admin profile. URL captured: https://admin-password-reset-7.preview.emergentagent.com/onboarding

After opening row menu (delete-menu-trigger-user-{id}) and clicking hard-delete-user-{id} ('Permanently delete…'), the AlertDialog with data-testid='force-hard-delete' does NOT render — count=0 in DOM. Either the alert dialog is not opening on the menu-item click, OR the force-hard-delete override button is gated behind a prior 409 dependency-conflict response and is not statically rendered as the review-request expects. Visibility-only check therefore fails.

integration_issues.issue: After signInWithPassword resolves, navigate('/') in Auth.tsx is intercepted by an AppShell/ProtectedRoute layer that re-routes to /onboarding. Direct GET /admin in a second navigation does NOT trigger that interception — proving the gate is inconsistent between '/' and '/admin' or only fires on the first post-login render.

action_items: URGENT: Fix the post-login redirect in /app/frontend/src/pages/Auth.tsx (line 55 area) and/or the ProtectedRoute / profile-completeness guard so that an already-Active Admin with a populated user_profiles row is NOT sent to /onboarding. Suggested checks in order: (a) ensure the guard reads user_profiles.status==='Active' OR profile_complete flag (whatever column drives onboarding) — confirm the column name matches what the SQL row exposes; the row JSON shows 'profile_comple…' truncated so the field likely exists; (b) verify the guard uses isAdmin/isManager from useAuth as a short-circuit; (c) ensure the guard isn't using a stale React-Query cache before user_roles + user_profiles resolve. The guard mis-fires only on the /' path (root) — /admin renders fine — so look at whichever index/dashboard route component decides to push to /onboarding.

Investigate DeleteEntityMenu.tsx: when an Admin clicks 'Permanently delete…' on a user row, ensure the AlertDialog opens AND that data-testid='force-hard-delete' is present in the dialog from first render (or document that it only appears after a 409 dependency response — in which case the review-request expectation needs adjusting OR the code needs to render the override button up-front for Admins).

Run the missing schema fixes: create public.login_attempts (or remove the frontend call) and add daily_solar_kwh / daily_grid_kwh columns to power_readings (or remove from the dashboard SELECT list). Both are non-blocking but throw 404/400 in the console on every load.

Re-test after fixes: login should redirect to '/' (or '/dashboard'), /admin should render directly, click delete-menu-trigger-user-{id} → click hard-delete-user-{id} → confirm force-hard-delete is visible, then Cancel.
critical_code_review_comments: Auth.tsx line 55 navigates to '/' after signInWithPassword without awaiting profile/roles fetch — this race plus an over-strict onboarding guard is the most likely cause of the /onboarding bounce. Recommend awaiting the user_profiles + user_roles fetch (or trusting the existing useAuth() loading state) before navigating, and gating the redirect on a single canonical 'profile_complete' boolean rather than inferring from individual fields.
ProtectedRoute / AppShell: the profile-complete gate must be identical across '/' and '/admin' routes — currently /admin lets the Admin in but '/' kicks them to /onboarding. Centralize the check.

DeleteEntityMenu.tsx (line ~308): if force-hard-delete is conditionally rendered, the review-request's visibility-only assertion will always fail without first invoking the delete and getting a 409. Either render the override button up-front for Admin (disabled state OK) or update the test contract.
Frontend Supabase queries reference a non-existent table (login_attempts) and non-existent columns (power_readings.daily_solar_kwh, daily_grid_kwh) — schema and code are out of sync.

success_rate.frontend: 40% — 3/5 review-request checks PASS (admin-page renders on direct nav, admin-access-denied not shown, Users tab enabled). 2/5 FAIL: post-login redirect goes to /onboarding (REGRESSION) and force-hard-delete button not visible after opening hard-delete menu.

test_credentials: kevzvilbar@gmail.com / BPWI2025! (Supabase Admin) — sign-in returns 200 with valid JWT (sub=vite-react-deploy).
seed_data_creation: None — no test data created or modified. No deletes executed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: The critical regression is purely client-side: Supabase data is GOOD (user_profiles.status='Active', designation='Admin', user_roles has Admin). Sign-in succeeds (200 JWT) and navigates away from /auth, but the post-login route '/' is then intercepted and pushed to /onboarding 'Complete your profile'. Direct /admin navigation works perfectly. Investigate /app/frontend/src/pages/Auth.tsx (line 55 navigate('/')) AND whichever index/'/' route or AppShell guard inspects user_profiles for completeness — the column it checks (likely 'profile_complete' / 'profile_completed_at') is probably either null or named differently than the guard expects despite the row clearly being a real, populated admin profile. Workaround for any further UI tests: after sign-in, page.goto('/admin') directly. For the force-hard-delete visibility, code reference line 308 of DeleteEntityMenu.tsx claims the testid exists, but on screen the AlertDialog after clicking 'Permanently delete…' did not contain it (count=0). Confirm whether it's gated on a 409 response or a force-toggle that needs to be flipped first.
rca of the issue: ROOT CAUSE (most probable): Auth.tsx calls navigate('/') immediately after supabase.auth.signInWithPassword resolves. The '/' index route (or its AppShell wrapper) then runs a profile-completeness guard that reads from user_profiles BEFORE useAuth() / react-query has hydrated isAdmin + the profile, OR it reads a column that is null/absent on this row even though the row IS complete. The guard pushes to /onboarding. Direct navigation to /admin uses Admin.tsx's own check (isManager from useAuth) which reads user_roles correctly and renders fine. REPRODUCTION: 1) GET /auth, sign in with kevzvilbar@gmail.com / BPWI2025!. 2) Observe URL becomes /onboarding within ~1s of submit. 3) Manually GET /admin — Admin console renders, all admin features (Users tab, Plants, Audit log, delete menu) are accessible. MITIGATION: temporarily, users can navigate to /admin directly. PERMANENT FIX: align the '/' index guard with /admin's guard (use useAuth().isAdmin / isManager OR the same single source of truth for profile completeness), and only redirect to /onboarding when useAuth().loading===false AND the profile genuinely lacks required fields.
